target.name: Cognition
target.founder: Scott Wu, Steven Hao, Walden Yan

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $3.2B - $39.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Figure AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $39B

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

data.narrative:
Cognition is a Series C AI & Software company founded by Scott Wu, Steven Hao, and Walden Yan. The company has raised $1.5B+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 9 private peers (including Figure AI, Skild AI, and Wayve) and 1 public comparable (Palantir, PLTR), all operating in applied AI.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $1.0B total-raised proxy as a baseline and applying a typical Series C step-up multiplier of 1.7x, implies a valuation of $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (Figure AI at $39.0B, Skild AI at $14.0B, Wayve at $8.6B, Physical Intelligence at $5.6B, and Black Forest Labs at $3.2B) yields a median of $8.6B, with a range spanning $3.2B to $39.0B. The wide dispersion reflects heterogeneous company maturity and product-market evidence within the cohort.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $9.9B (high), with a median of $5.2B. Key uncertainties include the reliability of stage-progression multiples in a compressed AI funding cycle and the appropriateness of peers with vastly different revenue traction and deployment scale. The peer-median approach carries material sensitivity to outliers (notably Figure AI's $39.0B valuation), warranting consideration of tighter bands if product differentiation or revenue metrics diverge materially from the peer set.
